workflow_name: Daily NYC Weather Summary with Freezing Alerts
description: Fetches daily weather data for New York City from Open-Meteo every morning and sends a formatted email summary with prominent freezing temperature alerts.

trigger.description: Runs every day at 7:00 AM America/New_York time. Timezone is set directly on the scheduleTrigger node for self-contained operation independent of instance timezone.

steps.name: Daily 7AM Schedule Trigger
steps.description: Fires every morning at 7:00 AM America/New_York. Timezone is configured directly on the node so it is instance-timezone-independent.
steps.error_handling: No special handling needed — trigger failure means workflow never starts.

steps.name: Fetch NYC Weather from Open-Meteo
steps.description: Calls the Open-Meteo API to retrieve current daily weather for New York City (lat 40.7128, lon -74.0060), requesting temperature max/min, apparent temperature, precipitation (inches), wind speed, and weather code.
steps.error_handling: Retry up to 3 times with 2-second waits. If all retries fail, the global error handler fires and logs 'Weather data unavailable today' with timestamp.

steps.name: Parse and Format Weather Summary
steps.description: Parses the Open-Meteo response with full null/undefined guards and date validation, maps weather codes to descriptions, checks for freezing conditions, builds fully-formatted HTML email body with prominent freeze warnings and a date-mismatch warning banner when applicable. Subject line reflects hard freeze vs standard freeze severity. Validates that daily.time[0] matches today's date in America/New_York timezone and surfaces a visible warning in the email if there is a mismatch.
steps.error_handling: Throws descriptive errors for missing/malformed API response fields. Renders a visible yellow warning banner in the email body if the API date does not match today's NYC date so the recipient is immediately aware of potential stale data.

steps.name: Send NYC Weather Email
steps.description: Sends the formatted HTML weather email via SendGrid API. The Authorization header is supplied by an n8n 'HTTP Header Auth' credential (credential name: SendGrid API Key) which stores the full 'Bearer <YOUR_SENDGRID_API_KEY>' value — no raw API key expressions in the node. Recipient and sender emails are read from n8n environment variables WEATHER_RECIPIENT_EMAIL and WEATHER_SENDER_EMAIL so they can be configured without editing the workflow JSON.

credential.name: SendGrid API Key
credential.note: Create this credential in n8n as HTTP Header Auth. Set header name to 'Authorization' and value to 'Bearer YOUR_SENDGRID_API_KEY'.

steps.error_handling: Retry twice with 2-second waits. A 401 response indicates the SendGrid HTTP Header Auth credential is misconfigured (wrong key or missing 'Bearer ' prefix). A 400 response indicates malformed body or missing required fields. Global error handler notifies developer on failure.

error_handling.on_workflow_failure: Log error with timestamp. Notify developer via secondary channel if possible, including a clear message such as 'Weather data unavailable today — workflow failed at [step name]' so the absence of the daily email is explained.

output.destination: Email inbox (configured via WEATHER_RECIPIENT_EMAIL environment variable)
output.description: Daily HTML email with NYC weather summary table; freeze/frost alerts displayed as colored banners at the top with bold text and emoji; a yellow warning banner is shown if the API date does not match today's NYC date; subject line reflects alert severity (Hard Freeze Warning > Freeze Alert > Wind Chill Freeze Alert > Frost Risk > normal). Temperature at 7AM is labeled clearly to avoid confusion with daily high/low forecasts.

credentials_needed: SendGrid API Key: Create an n8n 'HTTP Header Auth' credential named 'SendGrid API Key'. Set header name to 'Authorization' and value to 'Bearer YOUR_SENDGRID_API_KEY'. Do NOT paste the key into the node expression directly.
WEATHER_RECIPIENT_EMAIL: Set as an n8n environment variable. The email address that will receive the daily weather summary.
WEATHER_SENDER_EMAIL: Set as an n8n environment variable. A SendGrid-verified sender email address.
security.pii_handling: No PII processed. Only weather data and static/env-configured email addresses.
security.data_flow: Open-Meteo API → n8n (transform) → SendGrid API. No data stored persistently.

required_environment_variables.name: WEATHER_RECIPIENT_EMAIL
required_environment_variables.description: Email address to receive the daily NYC weather summary. Must be set before first run.

required_environment_variables.name: WEATHER_SENDER_EMAIL
required_environment_variables.description: Verified sender email address in your SendGrid account. Must be set before first run.

required_credentials.name: SendGrid API Key
required_credentials.note: The full 'Bearer ' prefix must be included in the credential value.

cost_estimate: $0.00 per run (Open-Meteo is free; SendGrid free tier covers 100 emails/day). Effectively $0/month for this use case.
timezone_requirements: The cron trigger has timezone: America/New_York set directly on the node rule, making it self-contained and independent of the n8n instance timezone setting. No instance-level timezone configuration is required.

test_cases.name: Happy path — mild day, no freeze alert

expected.subject: 🌆 NYC Weather — NYC Weather: High 65°F / Low 48°F | Mainly clear 🌤️

test_cases.name: Freezing alert — temperature at or below 32°F but above 28°F

expected.subject: ❄️ FREEZE ALERT — NYC Weather: High 33°F / Low 30°F | Slight snow 🌨️

test_cases.name: Hard freeze warning — temperature at or below 28°F

expected.subject: 🚨 HARD FREEZE WARNING — NYC Weather: High 24°F / Low 14°F | Heavy snow ❄️🌨️

test_cases.name: Wind chill freeze — air temp above 32 but feels below freezing

expected.subject: 🥶 WIND CHILL FREEZE ALERT — NYC Weather: High 40°F / Low 34°F | Overcast ☁️
⚠️ FROST RISK — NYC Weather: High 48°F / Low 35°F | Partly cloudy ⛅

test_cases.name: Malformed API response — missing daily field
Malformed API response — empty daily arrays
Date mismatch — API returns different date than today
Missing WEATHER_RECIPIENT_EMAIL env var — SendGrid rejects request

expected.note: SendGrid will return 400 Bad Request if the to.email field is empty or missing.
Indicates the HTTP Header Auth credential has a wrong or missing API key / Bearer prefix.
